commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
Adds a new `public/llms.txt` static map for ClawHub registry pages, docs, API references, CLI workflows, trust/security docs, and source-code entry points.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a static docs asset rather than a bug report. The PR body includes terminal proof that `/llms.txt` serves as `text/plain` from a candidate-branch local server.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can update `public/llms.txt` to remove stale sync references and rerun focused text checks without a product decision.

target.title: docs: add llms.txt
target.body:
## Summary

Adds `public/llms.txt` for `https://clawhub.ai/llms.txt`.

The file gives LLMs and agents a curated map of ClawHub: primary registry surfaces, canonical docs, API references, CLI workflows, trust/security docs, and source-code entry points.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `bun run ci:static`
- Confirmed `public/llms.txt` is ASCII text: `file public/llms.txt`
- Confirmed there are no stale Souls references: `rg -n "souls|soul-format|SOUL|soul" public/llms.txt` returned no matches
- Confirmed no legacy package web routes remain: `rg -n "https://clawhub.ai/packages|https://clawhub.ai/packages/new|/packages/<" public/llms.txt` returned no matches
- Spot-checked current source/docs routes for the linked ClawHub surfaces, including skills, plugins, publishers, search, publish routes, HTTP API docs, CLI docs, moderation docs, and security audit docs
- Prior GitHub CI on the rebased branch passed `static`, `unit`, `types-build`, `packages`, `e2e-http`, `playwright-smoke`, `playwright-local-auth`, secret scan, and Vercel Agent Review; current head is `f424d7b2f11eb26d6fb998c82076aa93e534fc2b`

Served-file proof from the candidate branch:

```text
$ VITE_CONVEX_URL=https://example.invalid VITE_CONVEX_SITE_URL=https://example.invalid bun run dev -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 4179
VITE v8.0.16 ready
Local: http://127.0.0.1:4179/

$ curl -fsS -D - http://127.0.0.1:4179/llms.txt | sed -n '1,32p'
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Vary: sec-fetch-dest, accept
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 16:12:55 GMT
Content-Length: 9177
Content-Type: text/plain
Last-Modified: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 16:11:55 GMT

# ClawHub

> ClawHub is the public registry for OpenClaw skills, plugins, and packages. Use it to discover, inspect, install, publish, update, and moderate agent-facing artifacts.

ClawHub is the registry layer for OpenClaw. Use native `openclaw` commands when installing skills or plugins into an OpenClaw workspace. Use the separate `clawhub` CLI for authenticated registry workflows such as login, publishing, sync, delete/undelete, transfer, package inspection, and moderation-aware diagnostics.

ClawHub pages and APIs expose live registry state: owner, slug or package name, versions, tags, changelogs, source attribution, files, downloads, stars, install counts, scan summaries, moderation status, and canonical URLs. Security audit results are useful trust signals, not guarantees. Before recommending an install, inspect the artifact page, version, source, changelog, required credentials, and scan state.

This file is a map for language models and agents. It is not an access-control policy. For crawler rules, use `robots.txt`.

## Primary

- [ClawHub](https://clawhub.ai): Public registry app for browsing skills, plugin/package records, and publishers.
- [OpenClaw ClawHub docs](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub): Canonical public documentation for ClawHub users, publishers, API clients, and operators.
- [OpenClaw docs llms.txt](https://docs.openclaw.ai/llms.txt): Full OpenClaw documentation map, including the ClawHub documentation section.
- [Markdown docs export](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub.md): Clean Markdown export of the ClawHub overview. Append `.md` to any docs page URL for the Markdown version.
- [OpenAPI v1](https://clawhub.ai/api/v1/openapi.json): Machine-readable API schema for ClawHub v1 endpoints.

## Public Registry Surfaces

- [Skills](https://clawhub.ai/skills): Browse and search skill bundles centered on `SKILL.md`.
- [Plugins and packages](https://clawhub.ai/plugins): Browse and search OpenClaw plugin/package records, including code plugins and bundle plugins.
- [Publishers](https://clawhub.ai/publishers): Browse user and organization publishers.
- [Skill publish](https://clawhub.ai/skills/publish): Web publishing flow for new skill versions.
- [Plugin/package publish](https://clawhub.ai/plugins/publish): Web publishing flow for plugin package records.

$ curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:4179/llms.txt | rg -n "clawhub.ai/packages|clawhub.ai/plugins|/packages/<|HTTP API"
22:- [Plugins and packages](https://clawhub.ai/plugins): Browse and search OpenClaw plugin/package records, including code plugins and bundle plugins.
25:- [Plugin/package publish](https://clawhub.ai/plugins/publish): Web publishing flow for plugin package records.
32:- [Plugin/package page pattern](https://clawhub.ai/plugins): Plugin package detail pages use `/plugins/<name>` for unscoped packages and `/plugins/<scope>/<name>` for scoped packages.
47:- [HTTP API](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/http-api): Detailed HTTP API reference for public, CLI, package, plugin, and auth routes.
80:- [Convex backend](https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub/tree/main/convex): Database schema, HTTP API handlers, auth, publishing, search, downloads, telemetry, security scans, moderation, packages, plugins, and publishers.
```

Vercel preview deployment is still blocked by team authorization, not by this diff.
target.author: vyctorbrzezowski

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: Pick-cat

target.title: fix(memory-core): index memory path in FTS text for filename queries (fixes #94102)
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #94102.
- `memory_search` FTS rows indexed only chunk body text, so queries for date-like filename stems (e.g. `2026-06-17-1649`) returned unrelated files with `textScore: 0` even when the target file was indexed and content search worked.
- Include the memory file path in the FTS `text` column at index time (`${entry.path}\n${chunk.text}`) so filename/date tokens participate in hybrid keyword ranking.
- Vector embeddings and chunk body text are unchanged.
- Keyword-search snippets and lexical density scoring strip the indexed path prefix back off, so excerpts show the chunk body (not the path line) and stay consistent with vector-search snippets; the FTS index keeps the path purely for matching.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Exact filename/date-token queries fail to rank the matching memory file first despite a clean index and working content search.
- Real environment tested: Linux (WSL2) source checkout, Node 22, FTS-only memory backend (`provider: none`, vector disabled).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Built the local checkout (`pnpm build`).
  2. Ran a redacted behavior probe that indexes two memory files and executes `memory_search` for `2026-06-17-1649` through the bundled memory-core runtime.
- Evidence after fix:
  Terminal transcript:
  ```text
  $ node behavior-probe-memory-filename.mjs
  {
    "top": [
      {
        "path": "memory/2026-06-17-1649.md",
        "textScore": 0.000003905989965114915,
        "score": 1
      }
    ]
  }
  ```

  Snippet-cleanliness guard (run via `node`), asserting the keyword hit returns the chunk body and not the `memory/...` path line:

  ```text
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.test.ts
  ✓ searchKeyword filename token ranking (issue #94102) > matches date-like filename stems when path is included in FTS indexed text
    expect(results[0].snippet).toBe("Token has been expired or revoked Google OAuth Testing mode")
    expect(results[0].snippet.startsWith("memory/")).toBe(false)
  Tests  23 passed (23)
  ```
- Observed result after fix: Top result path is `memory/2026-06-17-1649.md` with `textScore > 0`; nearby file `memory/2026-06-17-1701.md` is not returned for the filename stem query. The returned snippet for that hit is the chunk body (`Token has been expired or revoked Google OAuth Testing mode`), not the path-prefixed FTS text.
- What was not tested: Live `openclaw memory search` with Gemini embeddings against a production workspace (requires provider credentials and reindex).

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.test.ts` — 23 passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:test:extensions` — exit 0.
- Regression coverage: filename/date stem ranks first with `textScore > 0`, and the keyword snippet equals the chunk body (asserts it does not start with the `memory/` path prefix).

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No
Highest-risk area:
Hybrid memory search keyword ranking for existing indexes
Mitigation:
Change applies on reindex/write paths only; no config surface. Existing indexes need reindex to pick up path tokens in FTS text.

## Current review state

Next action:
Await CI + ClawSweeper review
Waiting on:
Maintainer review
Bot comments addressed:
Updated Real behavior proof after initial mock-only-proof gate failure

AI-assisted: yes
target.author: pick-cat
